attributes.description: Alerts when active queries run longer than 5 minutes (`db.server.query_sample`). Long queries may block others.
attributes.name: [PostgreSQL OTel] Long-running queries

investigation_guide.blob:
## PostgreSQL long-running queries

### What fired
Active query sample with `total_exec_time > 300000 ms` (5 min).

### Why it matters
Long-running queries hold locks, consume memory, and block VACUUM. Occasional long queries are expected (reports, maintenance); unexpected ones need intervention.

### Triage
1. Inspect the query and its plan.
2. Check wait events for the session.

### Remediation
- Kill rogue queries.
- Add timeouts (`statement_timeout`).

### Tuning
- 5 min threshold; tune to SLA.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM logs-postgresqlreceiver.otel-*
// Active query samples from pg_stat_activity; total_exec_time is real-time execution ms
| WHERE event_name == "db.server.query_sample"
| WHERE attributes.postgresql.total_exec_time IS NOT NULL
// Threshold: queries running > 5 minutes (300000 ms); adjust for your SLA
| WHERE attributes.postgresql.total_exec_time > 300000
| KEEP service.instance.id, attributes.db.namespace, attributes.db.query.text, attributes.postgresql.total_exec_time, attributes.postgresql.pid
| SORT attributes.postgresql.total_exec_time DESC
| LIMIT 20
